revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Araogawa Shrine (Ōsaki City) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden of Araogawa-jinja shrine Satomiya.JPG | location = {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|qid=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki City|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}}, Iwadeyama Ikeduki Aza Kamimiyashita 12 | coordinates = {{Coord|38.717194444444445|N|140.82044444444443|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Sēoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sēoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} | Rank = Gōsha
{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Established = (Traditionally) {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 4 (720) | Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri | Other names = Satomiya, Thirty-Six Shrine Myōjin, Thirty-Six Shrine Sama | festival = {{ill|August 7|en|August 7|qid=Q2781|lt=August 7th|1=August 7}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=43|lat_sec=1.9|lon_deg=140|lon_min=49|lon_sec=13.6|label=Araogawa Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Osaki|qid=Q385892|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}} | deity = {{ill|Seoritsuhime|qid=Q13579836|1=Seoritsu-hime}}; {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} '''Araogawa Shrine''' (荒雄川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|qid=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki City|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, Japan. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Gōsha. It is one of the three shrines in {{ill|Tamatsukuri District, Miyagi|en|Tamatsukuri District, Miyagi|qid=Q7680960|lt=Tamatsukuri District|1=Tamatsukuri District, Miyagi}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The name "Araogawa" is another term for the {{ill|Eai River|ja|江合川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eai River|1=Eai River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11550788}}. == Deities == * The main deities are {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Sēoritsuhime no Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sēoritsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}. *** According to the {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Shrine Association|ja|宮城県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Shrine Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinjachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590703}}, the primary deity is {{ill|Ōmononushi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ōmonoimi no Kami|qid=Q3270544}}. However, shrine history and discussions with the chief priest suggest that Ōmononushi is not enshrined at Satomiya. * Additional deities enshrined in auxiliary shrines include: ** {{ill|Yama Shrine|ja|山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yama Shrine|1=Mountain Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11470798}} (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) ** {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443279}} (deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}) ** {{nihongo|'''Mikumari-sha'''|水分神}} (deity: {{ill|Mikuma-no-Kami|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuma-no-Kami|qq=}}) == History == According to the shrine’s history, it was founded in {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō 4|1=Yōrō}} (720). It is one of the three {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} shrines in Tamatsukuri District. Araogawa Shrine in {{ill|Naruko Onsen|ja|鳴子温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruko Onsen|1=Naruko Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675321}}, another {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, serves as a counterpart to this one. The Iwadeyama Ikeduki shrine is referred to as "Satomiya" (lower shrine), while the Naruko Onsen shrine is "Takemiya" (upper shrine), with different priestly families managing each. The main deities of Satomiya are Susanoo no Mikoto and Sēoritsuhime no Mikoto. Sēoritsuhime, known for purifying defilements, is worshipped as a water deity controlling the upper reaches of Araogawa. The reverence for the name "Ōmononushi no Kami" likely stems from the association with the river's pure flow and the prohibition against polluting the waters. The shrine grounds have long been sacred, featuring Jōmon-period ritual sites known as the Araogawa Shrine Ruins. Artifacts like pottery and tools have been excavated. During the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|qid=Q1187448|lt=Zenkunen War|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, the military governor {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} offered prayers and treasures, such as golden blades. In the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|qid=Q1187411|lt=Gōsanen War|1=Gosannen War}}, General {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} also prayed for victory. In {{ill|Kaō (era)|en|Kaō (era)|qid=Q1150842|lt=Kaō 2|1=Kaō (era)}} (1170), {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidehira|en|Fujiwara no Hidehira|qid=Q3090625|lt=Fujiwara no Hidehira|1=Fujiwara no Hidehira}} designated Araogawa Shrine as "Oshū’s First Shrine." The shrine received strong support from {{ill|Ōsaki氏|ja|大崎氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki氏|1=Ōsaki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2975417}}, the regional lords during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period|1=Muromachi period}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine became the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Iwatesan Date Clan|ja|岩出山伊達家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatesan Date Clan|1=Iwatesan Date Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30922189}}. == Grounds == The shrine grounds include: * Main Shrine Hall (Haiden, Heiden, Honden) * {{ill|Yama Shrine|ja|山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yama Shrine|1=Mountain Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11470798}} for child blessings * {{ill|Tenjin Shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443279}} for academic success * {{nihongo|'''Mikumari-sha'''|水分神}} for water blessings * A Frog-shaped Stone Shrine for safe travels and recoveries * Remains of Jōmon-period ritual sites File:Torii gate of Araogawa-jinja shrine Satomiya.JPG|Torii gate File:Precincts of Araogawa-jinja shrine Satomiya.JPG|Shrine precincts File:Haiden of Araogawa-jinja shrine Satomiya in Shinto ritual.JPG|Haiden during a festival File:Honden of Araogawa-jinja shrine Satomiya.JPG|Honden File:Yama-jinja shrine at Araogawa-jinja shrine Satomiya.JPG|Yama Shrine File:Tenjinsha shrine at Araogawa-jinja shrine Satomiya.JPG|Tenjin Shrine File:Mikumari-sha shrine and Kaeru-no-Yashiro shrine(Small shrine of Frog) at Araogawa-jinja shrine Satomiya.JPG|Mikumari Shrine and Frog Stone Shrine File:Jomon period's religious site of Araogawa-jinja shrine Satomiya.JPG|Jōmon-period ritual site == References == * Shrine history from Araogawa Shrine Office == See Also == * {{ill|Araogawa Shrine (Naruko Onsen)|ja|荒雄川神社 (大崎市鳴子温泉鬼首)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araogawa Shrine (Naruko Onsen)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020459 Official Page - Miyagi Shrine Association] {{Shinto|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Omonoimi Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|荒雄川神社 (大崎市岩出山池月)|version=84089981|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Araogawa Shrine (Osaki City Iwadeyama Ikezuki) (Q11618878)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Araogawa Shrine (Osaki City Iwadeyama Ikezuki)|lt=Araogawa Shrine|ja|荒雄川神社 (大崎市岩出山池月)|qid=Q11618878|1=Araogawa Shrine (Osaki City Iwadeyama Ikezuki)}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || — || {{ill|Arawokahano Shrine|lt=Arawokahano Shrine|qid=Q135039992|1=Arawokahano Shrine}} || {{coord|38.717194|140.820444|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182060 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11618878}} [[da:d:Q11618878]] [[ja:荒雄川神社 (大崎市岩出山池月)]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]]==Notes== [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:大崎市の建築物]] [[Category:大崎市の歴史]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]]
